note: Single-pass world map proof — flux native gen, sips upscaled to 2k. Strict orthographic top-down for 2D walkability. Eyeball in the PROBE/WORLD viewer. Reroll if layout doesn't serve the arc.

sprites.name: world_proof
sprites.prompt: overhead map asset for a 2D top-down adventure game, strict orthographic projection, camera pointing straight down at 90 degrees, flat tabletop-RPG battle-map style like Stardew Valley overworld or a D&D map tile, uniform angle across the entire image, no perspective, no horizon, no sky, no clouds, no parallax, no fisheye, all features drawn as flat footprints from directly above. Single cohesive biome filling the entire map: muted-green ruined grassland with patches of dead yellow-brown grass, scattered small weathered concrete rubble fragments and stone slabs, sparse low shrubs and clumps of wildflowers, a single soft dirt path meandering gently from west to east through the middle. Far left edge gradually transitions into a soft sandy beach strip (leftmost ~15% of the image) and then a calm shallow ocean along the left edge with gentle wave foam where sand meets water — the transition is gradual, no hard line. Layout built for walkability: large open traversable plains in the center, obstacles clustered along edges and corners leaving wide horizontal and vertical corridors through the middle. Muted earth-tone palette of soft greens, tans, and warm browns with the ocean a calm desaturated teal. Sharp crisp detail, high contrast ground texture, clean hard edges on every element, no blur, no soft focus, no depth of field, no atmospheric haze, no glow, no bloom. No biome stripes, no hard vertical seams, no 3D buildings, no towers in side-view, no bridges, no overpasses, no elevation changes, no cliffs, no grid, no border, no vignette, no text, no UI.

sprites.name: world_desert
sprites.prompt: overhead map asset for a 2D top-down adventure game, strict orthographic projection, camera pointing straight down at 90 degrees, flat tabletop-RPG battle-map style like Stardew Valley overworld or a D&D map tile, uniform angle across the entire image, no perspective, no horizon, no sky, no clouds, no parallax, no fisheye, all features drawn as flat footprints from directly above. Single cohesive biome filling the entire map: sun-baked tan desert wastes with cracked dry earth, drifts of fine sand, scattered small rusted-metal debris and weathered concrete fragments, sparse dry brush and tumbleweed clumps, occasional bleached bone fragments, a single soft wind-blown trail meandering gently from west to east through the middle. Far bottom edge gradually transitions into a soft sandy beach strip (bottom ~15% of the image) and then a calm shallow warm ocean along the bottom edge with gentle wave foam where sand meets water — the transition is gradual, no hard line. Layout built for walkability: large open traversable flats in the center, obstacles clustered along edges and corners leaving wide horizontal and vertical corridors through the middle. Warm palette of tans, rust browns, faded ochres and bleached yellows with the ocean a desaturated turquoise. Painterly pixel texture, hand-drawn ground detail. No biome stripes, no hard horizontal seams, no 3D buildings, no towers in side-view, no bridges, no overpasses, no elevation changes, no dunes drawn in side-view, no cliffs, no grid, no border, no vignette, no text, no UI.

sprites.name: world_alien
sprites.prompt: overhead map asset for a 2D top-down adventure game, strict orthographic projection, camera pointing straight down at 90 degrees, flat tabletop-RPG battle-map style like Stardew Valley overworld or a D&D map tile, uniform angle across the entire image, no perspective, no horizon, no sky, no clouds, no parallax, no fisheye, all features drawn as flat footprints from directly above. Single cohesive biome filling the entire map edge to edge with no coastline and no water boundary: alien-terraformed wasteland with deep-purple cracked ground, faint magenta vein lines tracing the cracks, scattered small magenta crystal clusters and pale bone-white spires drawn as flat footprints from above, sparse discrete round cyan bio-pools dotted through the terrain (round pool shapes, NOT a maze of webs), patches of dark fungal growth, a single faint worn track meandering gently from north to south through the middle. Layout built for walkability: large open traversable terrain in the center, obstacles clustered along edges and corners leaving wide horizontal and vertical corridors through the middle. Palette of dark purples, magenta accents, pale cyan glow and muted bone-white. Painterly pixel texture, hand-drawn ground detail. No coastline, no ocean, no beach, no water along any edge, no biome stripes, no hard seams, no 3D buildings, no spires in side-view, no bridges, no overpasses, no elevation changes, no cliffs, no grid, no border, no vignette, no text, no UI.

sprites.name: world_frozen
sprites.prompt: overhead map asset for a 2D top-down adventure game, strict orthographic projection, camera pointing straight down at 90 degrees, flat tabletop-RPG battle-map style like Stardew Valley overworld or a D&D map tile, uniform angle across the entire image, no perspective, no horizon, no sky, no clouds, no parallax, no fisheye, all features drawn as flat footprints from directly above. Single cohesive biome filling the entire map edge to edge with no coastline and no water boundary: frozen industrial ruins covered in packed snow and icy slush, scattered small rusted metal beams and collapsed concrete slabs poking through the snow, drifts of windblown snow, sparse dead frost-bitten shrubs and dark twisted branches, occasional patches of exposed cracked ice, a single faint footpath meandering gently from north to south through the middle. Layout built for walkability: large open traversable snowfields in the center, obstacles clustered along edges and corners leaving wide horizontal and vertical corridors through the middle. Cold palette of pale blue-whites, soft grays, rusted browns and dark slate. Painterly pixel texture, hand-drawn ground detail. No coastline, no ocean, no frozen lake along any edge, no biome stripes, no hard seams, no 3D buildings, no towers in side-view, no bridges, no overpasses, no elevation changes, no cliffs, no grid, no border, no vignette, no text, no UI.

sprites.name: world_forest
sprites.prompt: overhead map asset for a 2D top-down adventure game, strict orthographic projection, camera pointing straight down at 90 degrees, flat tabletop-RPG battle-map style like Stardew Valley overworld or a D&D map tile, uniform angle across the entire image, no perspective, no horizon, no sky, no clouds, no parallax, no fisheye, all features drawn as flat footprints from directly above. Single cohesive biome filling the entire map edge to edge with no coastline and no water boundary on any edge: dense overgrown jungle ruins with deep-green mossy ground, scattered mossy stone slabs and cracked concrete fragments half-swallowed by foliage, clumps of ferns and curling vines, dark undergrowth, a single calm narrow stream winding gently from north to south through the INTERIOR of the map (the stream stays inland, never touches any edge), with a soft dirt footpath running roughly parallel to the stream. Layout built for walkability: large open traversable forest clearings in the center, obstacles clustered along edges and corners leaving wide horizontal and vertical corridors through the middle. Palette of deep mossy greens, mossy browns, dark earth tones and a desaturated water-blue stream. Painterly pixel texture, hand-drawn ground detail. No coastline, no ocean, no biome stripes, no hard seams, no 3D buildings, no trees drawn in side-view (foliage is overhead canopy footprint only), no bridges, no overpasses, no elevation changes, no cliffs, no grid, no border, no vignette, no text, no UI.
